channel.title: Mikael Chew — Insights & Strategy
channel.description: Practical wisdom for direct selling leaders who want to think deeper, lead better, and build sustainably.

item.title: The Alliance Problem: How to Build With Other Leaders Without Destroying Each Other
item.description: Cross-line collaboration is one of the most powerful growth strategies in direct selling — and one of the most mishandled. Here is how to build alliances that create value instead of conflict.

item.title: The Terrain Has Changed: Why Yesterday's Playbook Is Losing You Tomorrow's Leaders
item.description: The direct selling landscape has fundamentally shifted. Millennial and Gen Z leaders want autonomy, transparency, and digital-first systems — not hotel meetings and motivational rallies.

item.title: The Passive Income Myth: What Direct Selling Really Pays You For
item.description: Passive income is the most overused promise in direct selling. Here is what the income actually pays you for — and why understanding this changes everything.

item.title: The 10-Year Test: How to Know If Your Direct Selling Career Will Last
item.description: Most people think about their first 90 days in direct selling. Here is a framework of five questions to evaluate whether your approach will still be working a decade from now.

item.title: Stop Copying Your Upline: Why Duplication Is Killing Your Team
item.description: The industry loves the word 'duplication.' But blind copying is why most teams plateau. Learn the difference between duplicating principles and duplicating methods.

item.title: The 5 Battles Every New Direct Selling Leader Must Win
item.description: The first two years are a battlefield. Most people who quit do so because they did not know which battles to fight. Here are the five that matter most.

item.title: Why Your Team Keeps Quitting (And What You're Not Seeing)
item.description: If your team keeps leaving, the problem might not be them. Here are the three silent killers of retention that most leaders never see coming.

item.title: The General's Dilemma: When to Lead From the Front vs. From Behind
item.description: When should you personally prospect, and when should you step back to develop others? The answer is a sliding scale, not a switch.

item.title: The Dual Perspective: What I Learned Moving From the Field to the Boardroom
item.description: Most direct selling trainers have only seen one side. After 8 years in the field and 15 in corporate management, here is what both sides get wrong — and right.

item.title: Why Most Network Marketing Leaders Are Busy, Not Strategic
item.description: You built the team. Revenue is growing. But somehow you are busier than ever. The problem is not your work ethic — it is your operating model.

item.title: Win the Right Way: Why Ethics Is Your Greatest Competitive Advantage
item.description: In an industry where hype and shortcuts are common, ethical leadership is not just moral — it is the most powerful long-term strategy you can adopt.
